name: AAV9-mTERT extended lifespan and healthspan markers in adult and old mice
summary: A mouse gene-therapy study anchors the strongest animal lifespan signal for telomerase restoration.
statement: Systemic AAV9 delivery of mouse TERT to adult and old mice improved multiple health and fitness markers and increased median lifespan without an observed increase in cancer.
population_or_model: Adult and old mice treated with AAV9-mTERT
time_horizon: Lifespan follow-up after adult or old-age treatment
quantitative_note: Median lifespan increased by 24% when treated at 1 year of age and by 13% when treated at 2 years of age; catalytically inactive TERT did not reproduce the benefit.
caveats: The evidence is in mice, not humans.
AAV vector, dose, immune response, tissue distribution, and long-term oncogenic risk remain major translation questions.
The absence of increased cancer in this mouse study is not enough to establish human safety.
